displayName: Enable Cloud Update for Microsoft 365 Apps
description: Enabling Cloud Update ensures that users receive Microsoft 365 Application updates at least once per month. Cloud Update provides enhanced controls and insights for application update deployment, streamlining the monitoring and rollout of Microsoft 365 Apps. It also keeps users up to date with the latest application features and enhancements, which can include important security fixes.
userImpact: This group of tasks enables Microsoft 365 Cloud Update for devices assigned to Current Channel or Monthly Enterprise Channel. User devices are mapped to servicing profiles and receive updates based on configurable profile rules. Insights on app health are sent to Microsoft 365 Apps Admin Center and Microsoft 365 Lighthouse.

managementTemplateSteps.displayName: Onboard to Microsoft 365 Apps inventory service
managementTemplateSteps.description: This task onboards the tenant to the Microsoft 365 Apps Admin Center Inventory Service, which provides insights into the state of Office installations on tenant devices. Inventory Service catalogs the add-ins installed on each device and identifies devices running older, unsupported builds of Office. Onboarding to Inventory Service is required to enable cloud updates. This may take up to 10 minutes to complete. If it is not complete after 10 minutes, deploy the task again.
managementTemplateSteps.userImpact: This group of tasks enables Microsoft 365 Cloud Update for devices assigned to Current Channel or Monthly Enterprise Channel. User devices are mapped to servicing profiles and receive updates based on configurable profile rules. Insights on app health are sent to Microsoft 365 Apps Admin Center and Microsoft 365 Lighthouse.

informationLinks.displayName: Overview of cloud update in the Microsoft 365 Apps admin center
Notifications your users see when you set an update deadline for Microsoft 365 Apps
Best practices from the field: Roadmap to modern management of Microsoft 365 Apps in the enterprise

lambdaDefinitions.value:
select case when
                    status IS NULL
                    then 'Not Provisioned'
                    else status
                    end
                    from (select json_extract(data,'$.value[0].featureProvisionStatus') as status from arg0)

managementTemplateSteps.displayName: Enable Cloud Update for Microsoft 365 Apps
managementTemplateSteps.description: This task enables cloud update management for devices assigned to either Current Channel or Monthly Enterprise Channel. Deploying this task ensures that customers receive security and functionality updates to Microsoft Office.
managementTemplateSteps.userImpact: This group of tasks enables Microsoft 365 Cloud Update for devices assigned to Current Channel or Monthly Enterprise Channel. User devices are mapped to servicing profiles and receive updates based on configurable profile rules. Insights on app health are sent to Microsoft 365 Apps Admin Center and Microsoft 365 Lighthouse.

informationLinks.displayName: Overview of cloud update in the Microsoft 365 Apps admin center
Notifications your users see when you set an update deadline for Microsoft 365 Apps

versions.name: DeviceInventory_ServicingProfile

lambdaDefinitions.value:
select id from
                            (SELECT 
                            json_extract(value, '$.id') as id, json_extract(value,'$.targetChannel') as targetChannel, json_extract(value,'$.active') as active 
                            from arg0, json_each(data) 
                            where targetChannel = 4)

select id from
                            (SELECT 
                            json_extract(value, '$.id') as id, json_extract(value,'$.targetChannel') as targetChannel, json_extract(value,'$.active') as active 
                            from arg0, json_each(data) 
                            where targetChannel = 1)

select id from
                            (SELECT 
                            json_extract(value, '$.id') as id, json_extract(value,'$.targetChannel') as targetChannel, json_extract(value,'$.active') as active 
                            from arg0, json_each(data) 
                            where targetChannel = 4)

select id from
                            (SELECT 
                            json_extract(value, '$.id') as id, json_extract(value,'$.targetChannel') as targetChannel, json_extract(value,'$.active') as active 
                            from arg0, json_each(data) 
                            where targetChannel = 1)

select case when
                            active IS NULL or active = 0 
                            then 'Inactive'
                            else 'Active'
                            end
                            from 
                            (SELECT 
                            json_extract(value, '$.id') as id, json_extract(value,'$.targetChannel') as targetChannel, json_extract(value,'$.active') as active 
                            from arg0, json_each(data) 
                            where targetChannel = 4)

select case when
                            active IS NULL or active = 0 
                            then 'Inactive'
                            else 'Active'
                            end
                            from 
                            (SELECT 
                            json_extract(value, '$.id') as id, json_extract(value,'$.targetChannel') as targetChannel, json_extract(value,'$.active') as active 
                            from arg0, json_each(data) 
                            where targetChannel = 1)

SELECT value from 
                            (SELECT json_extract(value, '$.name') as name1, json_extract(value, '$.value') as value,json_extract(value, '$.id') as id
                            from arg0, json_each(json_extract(data, '$.rulesList.valueRules')) 
                            where name1 like '%ServicingDeadline%')

versions.processedDeploymentAction: @sys.write([{Resource:"https://config.office.net/",SuccessLogLevel:2,ErrorLoglevel:2,RetryLogLevel:2,IncludeResponseBodyInException:true} PUT clients.config.office.net/ServiceProfile/v1.0/enableCloud \ [["MEC","Monthly"]] ],"enableCloudRsp") EOC @sys.write([{Resource:"https://config.office.net/",SuccessLogLevel:2,ErrorLoglevel:2,RetryLogLevel:2,IncludeResponseBodyInException:true} GET clients.config.office.net/ServiceProfile/v1.0/Profiles],"profilesRsp") EOC @sys.write([@getMECChannelProfileId([@sys.read("profilesRsp")])],"MECprofilesId") EOC @sys.write([@getCCChannelProfileId([@sys.read("profilesRsp")])],"CCprofilesId") EOC @sys.write([{Resource:"https://config.office.net/",SuccessLogLevel:2,ErrorLoglevel:2,RetryLogLevel:2,IncludeResponseBodyInException:true} GET clients.config.office.net/ServiceProfile/v1.0/[@sys.read("MECprofilesId")]/rules],"MECRulesRsp") EOC @sys.write([{Resource:"https://config.office.net/",SuccessLogLevel:2,ErrorLoglevel:2,RetryLogLevel:2,IncludeResponseBodyInException:true} GET clients.config.office.net/ServiceProfile/v1.0/[@sys.read("CCprofilesId")]/rules],"CCRulesRsp") EOC @sys.DEPLOYSERVICINGPROFILE([@sys.read("enableCloudRsp")],[@sys.read("MECRulesRsp")],[@sys.read("CCRulesRsp")],) EOC @sys.append([/result]) EOC
